❓ infra
❌ failure4.174.17-e2e-telco5g-ptp158minfra_other HIGH
CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: CI builder image registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (manifest unknown) (affects: deployment setup / linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet wait timeout)
• Evidence:

```

[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder

Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...

Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown

make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125

+ podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false

Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known

```

Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:

```

Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found

+ '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'

+ return 1

```

• Root Cause: The ptp-operator Dockerfile (FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses set +xe / set -x (without restoring -e), so make docker-build and podman push both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs make deploy pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the PtpOperatorConfig and the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the builder image rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
• Is it a flake? No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
• Suggested Fix: Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the ptp-operator Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore set -e after the credential-hiding set +e block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
• Affected tests: e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.17: confirmed — registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 returns manifest unknown
• 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 (missing); image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 (never built/pushed)


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

2026-06-27T06:00
⏳ pending4.184.18-e2e-telco5g-ptprunning...2026-06-29T19:00
❌ failure4.194.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp33minfra_other LOW
Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Telco lab cluster not provisioned / API server unreachable (affects: all job steps post-setup)
• Evidence:

```

scp: /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig: No such file or directory

(cluster-setup step, 2026-06-25T05:19:38Z, exit status 1)


ClusterVersion: Installing "" for :

clusteroperators are missing


dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

(gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs — all fail hitting 10.8.34.117:6443)

```

• Root Cause: The Ansible playbook fetch-kubeconfig.yml attempted to kcli scp root@ptpcimno-installer:/root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig but the file was absent, indicating the OCP installation on the baremetal lab cluster ptpcimno never completed. The API server at 10.8.34.117:6443 was unreachable for every subsequent step. The cluster-setup step exited with code 1 at 05:19:38Z — all downstream gather steps cascaded from this single infra failure. No PTP-operator code or tests ever ran.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — lab environment state issue, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient lab cluster provisioning failure; no code change triggered it.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if it recurs, inspect the ptpcimno lab cluster provisioning pipeline and verify the installer VM has a completed kubeconfig at /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig before CI jobs are scheduled against it.
• Affected tests: All steps — e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup (failed), gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs (all cascaded failures). Zero PTP e2e tests executed.


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — lab cluster not ready cascades into every subsequent step.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.19: Confirmed — lab cluster ptpcimno API unreachable, cluster-setup step failed.
• 4.21: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.
• 4.23: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.

Affected Images: None — image imports all succeeded (registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.19.0-0.nightly-2026-06-24-152413, 187 images imported cleanly). No image pull failures.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW

2026-06-25T05:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure4.204.20-e2e-telco5g-ptp171minfra_other` (majority) + `test_regression` (tgm) HIGH
Nine clock-sync tests fail due to log-stream unavailability; two tgm holdover tests fail due to a bad GNSS-disable method introduced in commit `7c915d9`.
11 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:


Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:



TL;DR: Nine clock-sync tests fail due to log-stream unavailability; two tgm holdover tests fail due to a bad GNSS-disable method introduced in commit 7c915d9.



Root Cause Groups:


• 9/11 failures — PTP daemon log stream unavailable (dualfollower ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, dualnicbc ×3 implied):

Two overlapping sub-causes produce identical could not find regex in log stream errors:

• Sub-cause A (dualfollower, likely dualnicbc): GetProfileLogID searches for a one-time startup log line (Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1) that has been rotated out of the container log buffer. With logReduce: false and ptp4l emitting ~16 summary lines/s plus phc2sys at another ~16/s (~6.4 KB/s), the 10 MiB OCP container log limit is exhausted in ≈26 minutes. The test suite ran ≈38 minutes after cluster setup, so the entry is gone before any test starts.
• Sub-cause B (dualnicbcha, likely contributing to dualnicbc): PTP sync was never established on the hardware (no Grand Master lock → no master offset output), compounded by transient Kubernetes API Unauthorized errors that dropped the log-streaming connection mid-run (context deadline exceeded variant).
• Both variants manifest as: first test gets context deadline exceeded (Pass 2 stream times out after 3 minutes), later tests get timedout waiting for matches.

• 1/11 failure — Prometheus unreachable (dualnicbcha, CPU-utilization test):

The test node could not reach the Prometheus endpoint during the CPU-utilization assertion; this is consistent with the broader environment/auth degradation seen in the same run.


• 2/11 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL enters FREERUN (class 248) instead of HOLDOVER (class 7) (tgm ×2):

disableGNSSViaSignalRequirements() uses ubxtool CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50 to block the navigation solution, but this leaves the DPLL's physical 1PPS reference intact. The DPLL therefore skips HOLDOVER and drops directly to FREERUN (clock class 248). This replaced a reliable coldboot method (ubxtool -p COLDBOOT) in commit 7c915d9 (Replace ublox coldboot with config for min required signal strength, Nov 17 2025, main).



Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteLog-stream failurePrometheus failureDPLL FREERUN
dualfollower✓ (3/3, log rotation)——
dualnicbc✓ (3/3, implied)——
dualnicbcha✓ (2/3, stream silent + auth)✓ (1/3)—
tgm——✓ (2/2)

• All three clock-sync suites share the same could not find regex in log stream failure signature; only the mechanism differs (log rotation vs. PTP sync never established).
• The auth/connectivity degradation (Unauthorized errors, dropped streams) is cross-suite infrastructure and likely explains why dualnicbc and dualnicbcha also see stream failures even if the startup line itself was present.
• The tgm failures are entirely independent — a test-code regression specific to GNSS-disable logic.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix log-rotation race (highest impact — unblocks 6–9 failures): Stop relying on the daemon's one-time startup log line as a test anchor. Either: (a) persist the profile-ID in a Kubernetes ConfigMap or annotation that the test reads via the API, or (b) restart the relevant linuxptp-daemon pod at the start of each suite to guarantee the startup entry is fresh. As a stopgap, set logReduce: true in the test PtpConfig to reduce log volume from ~32 lines/s to near-zero background noise, extending log retention beyond the test window.

2. Restore reliable GNSS disable for T-GM holdover tests (fixes 2 failures): Revert 7c915d9 or replace CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS with a method that actually forces DPLL into HOLDOVER — e.g., restore the ubxtool -p COLDBOOT approach or physically disconnect/reconnect the antenna cable in the hardware lab setup.

3. Investigate and fix environment/auth instability (reduces flakiness): The Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list errors from the CI operator indicate the cluster's API server credentials expired or the network degraded mid-run. Renew service-account tokens with a longer TTL, or add a pre-test credential-refresh step, to prevent the log-streaming API from being cut off during active tests.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (majority) + test_regression (tgm)

The 9 log-stream failures are driven by a combination of a fragile test design (relying on ephemeral startup logs) and environment degradation; the 2 tgm failures are a clear test-code regression.


Overall Severity: HIGH

Nine of eleven failures are likely to recur on every run that starts more than ~26 minutes after cluster bring-up, making the suite effectively non-functional in its current state.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 clock-sync tests time out because the linuxptp-daemon's startup configuration log entry (Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1) has been rotated out of the Kubernetes container log buffer before the tests attempt to read it, making the critical GetProfileLogID regex search permanently fail.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Daemon startup log unavailable due to container log rotation (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")

• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5c2rz container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5c2rz container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches

```

From junit-failures.txt and from all 3 dualfollower tests (identical pod linuxptp-daemon-5c2rz). Same pattern repeats on 4 other pods across dualnicbc and dualnicbcha suites.


The test binary ran against images built from release-4.20 with USE_UPSTREAM=false (confirmed in telco5g-ptp-tests__build-log.txt line 12: PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.20). The cluster setup completed at 05:45:53 UTC; the dualfollower suite started at 2026-06-29T06:24:04 — a ~38-minute gap.


• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID (in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69) searches the daemon's container logs for the multiline regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]. This string is printed exactly once per profile application by printNodeProfile (in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369). The ptpconfig is created with logReduce: false and ptp4lEthernetSlave = "-2 -s --summary_interval -4" (from test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go:65), which causes ptp4l to emit summary messages at 2⁻⁴ = 16 per second; phc2sys adds another ~16/s (configured with -R 16). Combined this generates roughly 32 lines/s × 200 bytes = ~6.4 KB/s per daemon pod, filling a typical OCP container log limit of ~10 MiB in ≈26 minutes. At 38 minutes post-deployment the startup message has been rotated out. GetPodLogsRegex (pods.go:214) attempts a 1-minute no-follow snapshot (Pass 1) then a 3-minute follow stream (Pass 2) — both fail because the entry is no longer in the accessible log buffer. The first test gets context deadline exceeded (Pass 2 context expired after 3 minutes of blocked reads); subsequent tests get timedout waiting for matches (the follow stream stays open but never produces the missing startup line).

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no specific SHA can be confirmed against release-4.20 from the available evidence. The logReduce: false entry in testconfig.go:1679 and summary_interval -4 in testconfig.go:64–65 drive the log verbosity that causes rotation; if either was recently changed on main (the test branch), it could be the trigger — but per branch-awareness rules, a main-only change cannot be the root cause unless cherry-picked. A recent release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon commit that increased daemon log verbosity or reduced logReduce enforcement would also explain it. Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate release-4.20 against recent ptp-operator and linuxptp-daemon commits touching log settings to confirm.

• Is it a flake? No — the failure is deterministic given the ~38-minute setup-to-test gap: startup logs rotate out after ≈26 minutes at this verbosity, so the window reliably closes before the clock-sync tests execute.

• Suggested Fix: Force a daemon config refresh at the start of each clock-sync test block (e.g., add a no-op update or delete/recreate the ptpconfig) so printNodeProfile logs a fresh startup entry. Alternatively, raise the ptp4l summary_interval to ≥ 0 in the test ptpconfig (or set logReduce: true) to reduce log volume below the rotation threshold, or pass SinceSeconds: 60 in GetPodLogsRegex Pass 2 and trigger a daemon restart if the startup message is absent.

Suite Issue Class: test_flake


Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbcha



Suite: dualnicbcha (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three failures are infrastructure-level — two clock-sync tests timed out because the linuxptp-daemon log stream produced no expected PTP-sync log lines, and one CPU-utilization test failed because Prometheus was unreachable on the test node.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP log stream silent / unreadable (affects: "Slave can sync to master [Serial]", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-2ggtf container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5vjbj container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

Two distinct error variants in the same run: context deadline exceeded (the Kubernetes API closed the streaming connection before any match was found) and timedout waiting for matches (the stream stayed open but produced zero matching lines within the 202 s test deadline). The same could not find regex in log stream error appears for the identical tests in the dualfollower and dualnicbc suites in the same job run (junit-failures.txt), making 9 clock-sync failures across 3 suites with the same signature.

• Root Cause: The linuxptp-daemon pods on the BC-master and slave nodes never produced the expected foreign master / master offset log lines that the test framework scans for via the Kubernetes pod log-streaming API. This is consistent with PTP sync never being established in the hardware lab during this run (no Grand Master lock → no MASTER\_CLOCK\_SELECTED event → no ptp4l offset output). The context deadline exceeded variant (daemon linuxptp-daemon-2ggtf) further suggests the k8s API server dropped the log-streaming connection, possibly due to a transient auth or network degradation: the CI operator log shows error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized) and repeated couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials errors immediately after the test phase ended. Note: linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1621 calls printWhenNotEmpty(logfilter.FilterOutput(p.logFilters, output)) — if logReduce is active in the deployed PtpConfig, master-offset lines are silently dropped from container stdout, which would produce the same symptom; however the cross-suite breadth of failures makes environment degradation the more likely primary cause.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the most recent upstream-sync commit in the cloned linuxptp-daemon repo is 0459bc0 (CNF-24996 / OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23, "Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026"), but it was not possible to confirm whether it is backported to release-4.20 (the check_backport.py script was not present). No specific code change is confirmed as causal.
• Is it a flake? yes — the identical error pattern appears for all 9 clock-sync tests across all three parallel suites in a single run, consistent with a transient lab/hardware environment degradation rather than a deterministic code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the periodic job; if the failure recurs, check whether the PTP Grand Master (GPS antenna / GNSS signal) was healthy at the time of the run and whether the kubeconfig token was still valid for the full duration of the 2+ hour test window.

• Group 2: Prometheus unreachable on cnfdf31 (affects: "Soak testing PTP CPU Utilization [parallel]")
• Evidence:

```

CPU usage query failed after 3 attempts: failed to get total cpu usage for ptp pods on node cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com: prometheus query failure: failed to get a (valid) response from prometheus

```

• Root Cause: The test queries the Prometheus API to measure PTP pod CPU consumption on cnfdf31; all three retry attempts returned no valid response. This is an independent monitoring-stack failure on node cnfdf31 — the Prometheus service was either down, unreachable from the test pod, or returning non-200 responses at the time the soak test ran. Given the concurrent auth failures visible in the CI operator log (the server has asked for the client to provide credentials), it is likely that the monitoring stack authentication or cluster networking was degraded during this run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change identified as causal.
• Is it a flake? yes — Prometheus-unreachable failures of this type are transient infrastructure flakes; the monitoring stack occasionally has availability gaps in the telco5g lab.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if persistent, check the Thanos/Prometheus route health on cnfdf31 and ensure the cluster monitoring stack's service-account tokens are valid for the full test duration.

Suite Issue Class: infra_timeout

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


tgm



Suite: tgm (2 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Both tgm holdover tests fail because the WPC T-GM hardware DPLL transitions directly to FREERUN (clock class 248) instead of HOLDOVER (clock class 7) when GNSS is disabled via the software navigation-filter method.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: DPLL enters FREERUN (class 248) instead of HOLDOVER (class 7) after GNSS disable (affects: "Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss", "Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery")

• Evidence:
• From junit-failures.txt line 82: Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial] → Message: Timed out waiting for clock class 7
• From junit-failures.txt lines 84-88: Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial] → Message: expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] / Expected : false to be true
• Test 1 uses waitForClockClass(fullConfig, "7") with a 3-minute timeout (test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:3509: if time.Since(start) > pkg.TimeoutIn3Minutes { Fail(...) })
• Test 2 uses verifyMetric(events[ptpEvent.PtpClockClassChange], float64(fbprotocol.ClockClass7)) but receives metric value 248

• Root Cause: Both tests call disableGNSSViaSignalRequirements() (test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:3456) which runs ubxtool -P 29.25 -w 1 -v 3 -z CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS,50,1 — this sets the u-blox navigation satellite count threshold to 50 (effectively impossible), blocking the software navigation solution. However, CFG-NAVSPG-INFIL_NCNOTHRS only filters th
2026-06-29T05:00
❓ infra
❌ failure4.214.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp305mtest_flake LOW
WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.
15 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: 13 of 15 failures are a single test flake — a log-rotation race that voids GetProfileLogID; 2 are a WPC T-GM holdover hardware failure.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID log-rotation timeout (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc): BasicClockSyncCheck → GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) opens a Follow: true log stream and searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[.*\]. That regex only matches a line emitted once by printNodeProfile at daemon startup. Cluster setup finished at ~02:06; ClockSync tests ran at ~04:00–06:00 (~2–4 h later). By then CRI-O log rotation had cycled the startup entry out of the K8s-served log buffer. The snapshot read yields nothing (~26 s); the follow-stream times out after 180 s — producing the observed ~206–218 s failure duration across every suite. The "Can provide a profile with higher priority" variant adds ~60–90 s of profile-apply wait before hitting the same timeout (yielding ~287–298 s).

• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL holdover not sustained (tgm): After GNSS disconnect, ts2phc either never emits the "holdover" log token (test 1 — 3+ min of retries, class stays at 6) or emits class-7 followed 7 ms later by class-248 (test 2 — DPLL flips through holdover and drops to FREERUN before the test can assert it). Root is in the WPC DPLL firmware or the ts2phc_parser.go:162-163 holdover-detection logic, not in the test itself.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualfollower3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbcha2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
oc2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
tgm2WPC T-GM holdover hardware/firmware failure

All five non-tgm suites share an identical root cause and near-identical failure durations. The dualnicbcha and oc per-suite analyses characterize the symptom as "LOCKED state timeout," but the timing signature (206–218 s, 287–298 s) and the GetProfileLogID call stack are identical to bc/dualfollower/dualnicbc. tgm is the only suite with a distinct, hardware-level root cause.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix GetProfileLogID to not depend on rotated startup logs. Instead of parsing the one-shot printNodeProfile startup line, query the current PTP configuration state directly — either from a /metrics endpoint, the PtpConfig CR status, or by triggering a daemon profile-reload (e.g., a no-op annotation update) immediately before the test assertion window. This single fix eliminates 13/15 failures.

2. Investigate the WPC DPLL holdover firmware behavior on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Capture ts2phc raw log output during GNSS disconnect to determine whether the DPLL hardware never enters holdover or enters and exits it within milliseconds. If the latter, check whether the DPLL holdover duration register is being set correctly from ts2phc.holdover config and whether a firmware update is available for the WPC card.

3. Add a canary assertion for daemon log reachability before ClockSync tests. A short pre-check that confirms GetPodLogsRegex can read at least one log line from the daemon container (not the specific startup pattern) would convert a silent 206 s timeout into a fast, labeled skip/infra-failure, making future regressions easier to triage.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (13/15); infra_other (2/15 tgm)


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dominant failure mode is a pre-existing, deterministic time-dependent flake (not a code regression); the tgm failures indicate a real hardware/firmware problem on one lab node but affect only 2 tests.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 bc ClockSync tests fail at the same step — GetProfileLogID cannot find the regex Ptp4lConf: #profile: ...message_tag [...] in the linuxptp-daemon container logs, because this log pattern only appears on a second profile application and the daemon had been stable for ~2 hours since cluster setup with no re-apply.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID regex never matches — daemon log pattern requires second profile apply (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[Jun 26 04:03:18.197][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-nqmpf container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[Jun 26 04:04:28.470][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, ...context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[Jun 26 04:05:39.315][ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-nqmpf container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Failure message (Test 1): Timed out after 202.124s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-bc-master1


Failure message (Test 2): Timed out after 202.701s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1 (pod linuxptp-daemon-2vqqd)


Failure message (Test 3): Timed out after 201.746s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp / err=timedout waiting for matches


Cluster setup completed at 02:06:48; ClockSync tests started at 04:02:12 (~1h55m later). The daemon applied profiles ONCE at setup startup and was stable since then.


• Root Cause: GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) uses the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\]. This pattern is only logged by printNodeProfile (daemon.go:893) when nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf already contains the RENDERED config (i.e., after configInput = configOutput at daemon.go:1119 has updated the pointer's string on a PREVIOUS apply). On the very first profile application, printNodeProfile logs the RAW CRD config which has NO #profile: header and NO message_tag line — both are added only by RenderPtp4lConf() / ExtendGlobalSection(). Since the daemon applied profiles once at cluster-setup time (~2 hours before tests) and never re-applied them, neither Pass 1 (existing logs) nor Pass 2 (follow stream) in GetPodLogsRegex (pods.go:214) can find the pattern. Test 3 (higher priority profile) also creates a brand-new temp profile that is applied exactly once, so the same structural mismatch applies.

• Breaking PR/Commit: The structural flaw is in GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) itself — no single commit is the direct trigger. The two-pass GetPodLogsRegex was introduced/modified in commits 0f98a60 and 9c1bc7f; 41f3dd9 (May 11 2026) changed the initial snapshot timeout from 3 min to 1 min but is not the root regression. The underlying issue — that Ptp4lConf: #profile: only appears in logs on the 2nd+ apply — has existed as a latent flake since GetProfileLogID was introduced. Unknown PR for the original introduction; the relevant release branch is release-4.21 in github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.

• Is it a flake? Yes — the test passes when something triggers a second profile application between cluster setup and the ClockSync test window (e.g., operator config reconciliation, hardware config event, daemon restart). In this run, ~2 hours of stability after setup meant no re-apply occurred.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileLogID, instead of relying on the Ptp4lConf: log (which requires a second apply), read the ptp4l config file directly from the pod filesystem (/var/run/ptp4l.0.config)


TL;DR: 13 of 15 failures are a single test flake — a log-rotation race that voids GetProfileLogID; 2 are a WPC T-GM holdover hardware failure.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID log-rotation timeout (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc): BasicClockSyncCheck → GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) opens a Follow: true log stream and searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[.*\]. That regex only matches a line emitted once by printNodeProfile at daemon startup. Cluster setup finished at ~02:06; ClockSync tests ran at ~04:00–06:00 (~2–4 h later). By then CRI-O log rotation had cycled the startup entry out of the K8s-served log buffer. The snapshot read yields nothing (~26 s); the follow-stream times out after 180 s — producing the observed ~206–218 s failure duration across every suite. The "Can provide a profile with higher priority" variant adds ~60–90 s of profile-apply wait before hitting the same timeout (yielding ~287–298 s).

• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL holdover not sustained (tgm): After GNSS disconnect, ts2phc either never emits the "holdover" log token (test 1 — 3+ min of retries, class stays at 6) or emits class-7 followed 7 ms later by class-248 (test 2 — DPLL flips through holdover and drops to FREERUN before the test can assert it). Root is in the WPC DPLL firmware or the ts2phc_parser.go:162-163 holdover-detection logic, not in the test itself.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualfollower3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbcha2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
oc2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
tgm2WPC T-GM holdover hardware/firmware failure

All five non-tgm suites share an identical root cause and near-identical failure durations. The dualnicbcha and oc per-suite analyses characterize the symptom as "LOCKED state timeout," but the timing signature (206–218 s, 287–298 s) and the GetProfileLogID call stack are identical to bc/dualfollower/dualnicbc. tgm is the only suite with a distinct, hardware-level root cause.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix GetProfileLogID to not depend on rotated startup logs. Instead of parsing the one-shot printNodeProfile startup line, query the current PTP configuration state directly — either from a /metrics endpoint, the PtpConfig CR status, or by triggering a daemon profile-reload (e.g., a no-op annotation update) immediately before the test assertion window. This single fix eliminates 13/15 failures.

2. Investigate the WPC DPLL holdover firmware behavior on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Capture ts2phc raw log output during GNSS disconnect to determine whether the DPLL hardware never enters holdover or enters and exits it within milliseconds. If the latter, check whether the DPLL holdover duration register is being set correctly from ts2phc.holdover config and whether a firmware update is available for the WPC card.

3. Add a canary assertion for daemon log reachability before ClockSync tests. A short pre-check that confirms GetPodLogsRegex can read at least one log line from the daemon container (not the specific startup pattern) would convert a silent 206 s timeout into a fast, labeled skip/infra-failure, making future regressions easier to triage.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (13/15); infra_other (2/15 tgm)


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dominant failure mode is a pre-existing, deterministic time-dependent flake (not a code regression); the tgm failures indicate a real hardware/firmware problem on one lab node but affect only 2 tests.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileLogID, instead of relying on the Ptp4lConf: log (which requires a second apply), read the ptp4l config file directly from the pod filesystem (/var/run/ptp4l.0.config)


TL;DR: 13 of 15 failures are a single test flake — a log-rotation race that voids GetProfileLogID; 2 are a WPC T-GM holdover hardware failure.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID log-rotation timeout (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc): BasicClockSyncCheck → GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) opens a Follow: true log stream and searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[.*\]. That regex only matches a line emitted once by printNodeProfile at daemon startup. Cluster setup finished at ~02:06; ClockSync tests ran at ~04:00–06:00 (~2–4 h later). By then CRI-O log rotation had cycled the startup entry out of the K8s-served log buffer. The snapshot read yields nothing (~26 s); the follow-stream times out after 180 s — producing the observed ~206–218 s failure duration across every suite. The "Can provide a profile with higher priority" variant adds ~60–90 s of profile-apply wait before hitting the same timeout (yielding ~287–298 s).

• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL holdover not sustained (tgm): After GNSS disconnect, ts2phc either never emits the "holdover" log token (test 1 — 3+ min of retries, class stays at 6) or emits class-7 followed 7 ms later by class-248 (test 2 — DPLL flips through holdover and drops to FREERUN before the test can assert it). Root is in the WPC DPLL firmware or the ts2phc_parser.go:162-163 holdover-detection logic, not in the test itself.

SuiteFailuresRoot Cause

dualfollower3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)

dualnicbcha2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
oc2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature

All five non-tgm suites share an identical root cause and near-identical failure durations. The dualnicbcha and oc per-suite analyses characterize the symptom as "LOCKED state timeout," but the timing signature (206–218 s, 287–298 s) and the GetProfileLogID call stack are identical to bc/dualfollower/dualnicbc. tgm is the only suite with a distinct, hardware-level root cause.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix GetProfileLogID to not depend on rotated startup logs. Instead of parsing the one-shot printNodeProfile startup line, query the current PTP configuration state directly — either from a /metrics endpoint, the PtpConfig CR status, or by triggering a daemon profile-reload (e.g., a no-op annotation update) immediately before the test assertion window. This single fix eliminates 13/15 failures.

2. Investigate the WPC DPLL holdover firmware behavior on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Capture ts2phc raw log output during GNSS disconnect to determine whether the DPLL hardware never enters holdover or enters and exits it within milliseconds. If the latter, check whether the DPLL holdover duration register is being set correctly from ts2phc.holdover config and whether a firmware update is available for the WPC card.

3. Add a canary assertion for daemon log reachability before ClockSync tests. A short pre-check that confirms GetPodLogsRegex can read at least one log line from the daemon container (not the specific startup pattern) would convert a silent 206 s timeout into a fast, labeled skip/infra-failure, making future regressions easier to triage.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (13/15); infra_other (2/15 tgm)


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dominant failure mode is a pre-existing, deterministic time-dependent flake (not a code regression); the tgm failures indicate a real hardware/firmware problem on one lab node but affect only 2 tests.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 bc ClockSync tests fail at the same step — GetProfileLogID cannot find the regex Ptp4lConf: #profile: ...message_tag [...] in the linuxptp-daemon container logs, because this log pattern only appears on a second profile application and the daemon had been stable for ~2 hours since cluster setup with no re-apply.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID regex never matches — daemon log pattern requires second profile apply (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[Jun 26 04:03:18.197][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-nqmpf container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[Jun 26 04:04:28.470][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, ...context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[Jun 26 04:05:39.315][ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-nqmpf container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Failure message (Test 1): Timed out after 202.124s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-bc-master1


Failure message (Test 2): Timed out after 202.701s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1 (pod linuxptp-daemon-2vqqd)


Failure message (Test 3): Timed out after 201.746s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp / err=timedout waiting for matches


Cluster setup completed at 02:06:48; ClockSync tests started at 04:02:12 (~1h55m later). The daemon applied profiles ONCE at setup startup and was stable since then.


• Root Cause: GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) uses the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\]. This pattern is only logged by printNodeProfile (daemon.go:893) when nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf already contains the RENDERED config (i.e., after configInput = configOutput at daemon.go:1119 has updated the pointer's string on a PREVIOUS apply). On the very first profile application, printNodeProfile logs the RAW CRD config which has NO #profile: header and NO message_tag line — both are added only by RenderPtp4lConf() / ExtendGlobalSection(). Since the daemon applied profiles once at cluster-setup time (~2 hours before tests) and never re-applied them, neither Pass 1 (existing logs) nor Pass 2 (follow stream) in GetPodLogsRegex (pods.go:214) can find the pattern. Test 3 (higher priority profile) also creates a brand-new temp profile that is applied exactly once, so the same structural mismatch applies.

• Breaking PR/Commit: The structural flaw is in GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) itself — no single commit is the direct trigger. The two-pass GetPodLogsRegex was introduced/modified in commits 0f98a60 and 9c1bc7f; 41f3dd9 (May 11 2026) changed the initial snapshot timeout from 3 min to 1 min but is not the root regression. The underlying issue — that Ptp4lConf: #profile: only appears in logs on the 2nd+ apply — has existed as a latent flake since GetProfileLogID was introduced. Unknown PR for the original introduction; the relevant release branch is release-4.21 in github.com/openshift/ptp-operator.

• Is it a flake? Yes — the test passes when something triggers a second profile application between cluster setup and the ClockSync test window (e.g., operator config reconciliation, hardware config event, daemon restart). In this run, ~2 hours of stability after setup meant no re-apply occurred.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileLogID, instead of relying on the Ptp4lConf: log (which requires a second apply), read the ptp4l config file directly from the pod filesystem (/var/run/ptp4l.0.config)


TL;DR: 13 of 15 failures are a single test flake — a log-rotation race that voids GetProfileLogID; 2 are a WPC T-GM holdover hardware failure.



Root Cause Groups:


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID log-rotation timeout (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc): BasicClockSyncCheck → GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:69) opens a Follow: true log stream and searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[.*\]. That regex only matches a line emitted once by printNodeProfile at daemon startup. Cluster setup finished at ~02:06; ClockSync tests ran at ~04:00–06:00 (~2–4 h later). By then CRI-O log rotation had cycled the startup entry out of the K8s-served log buffer. The snapshot read yields nothing (~26 s); the follow-stream times out after 180 s — producing the observed ~206–218 s failure duration across every suite. The "Can provide a profile with higher priority" variant adds ~60–90 s of profile-apply wait before hitting the same timeout (yielding ~287–298 s).

• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL holdover not sustained (tgm): After GNSS disconnect, ts2phc either never emits the "holdover" log token (test 1 — 3+ min of retries, class stays at 6) or emits class-7 followed 7 ms later by class-248 (test 2 — DPLL flips through holdover and drops to FREERUN before the test can assert it). Root is in the WPC DPLL firmware or the ts2phc_parser.go:162-163 holdover-detection logic, not in the test itself.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualfollower3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbc3Log rotation (GetProfileLogID)
dualnicbcha2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
oc2Log rotation (GetProfileLogID) — same 206–218 s signature
tgm2WPC T-GM holdover hardware/firmware failure

All five non-tgm suites share an identical root cause and near-identical failure durations. The dualnicbcha and oc per-suite analyses characterize the symptom as "LOCKED state timeout," but the timing signature (206–218 s, 287–298 s) and the GetProfileLogID call stack are identical to bc/dualfollower/dualnicbc. tgm is the only suite with a distinct, hardware-level root cause.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix GetProfileLogID to not depend on rotated startup logs. Instead of parsing the one-shot printNodeProfile startup line, query the current PTP configuration state directly — either from a /metrics endpoint, the PtpConfig CR status, or by triggering a daemon profile-reload (e.g., a no-op annotation update) immediately before the test assertion window. This single fix eliminates 13/15 failures.

2. Investigate the WPC DPLL holdover firmware behavior on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Capture ts2phc raw log output during GNSS disconnect to determine whether the DPLL hardware never enters holdover or enters and exits it within milliseconds. If the latter, check whether the DPLL holdover duration register is being set correctly from ts2phc.holdover config and whether a firmware update is available for the WPC card.

3. Add a canary assertion for daemon log reachability before ClockSync tests. A short pre-check that confirms GetPodLogsRegex can read at least one log line from the daemon container (not the specific startup pattern) would convert a silent 206 s timeout into a fast, labeled skip/infra-failure, making future regressions easier to triage.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (13/15); infra_other (2/15 tgm)


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dominant failure mode is a pre-existing, deterministic time-dependent flake (not a code regression); the tgm failures indicate a real hardware/firmware problem on one lab node but affect only 2 tests.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileLogID, instead of relying on the Ptp4lConf: log (which requires a second apply), read the ptp4l config file directly from the pod filesystem (/var/run/ptp4l.0.config)
